question: Which artist has won the most Grammy Awards in history?
explanation: Beyoncé holds the all-time record with 32 Grammy wins.

question: Which band released the album 'Dark Side of the Moon'?
explanation: Pink Floyd released it in 1973. It spent over 950 weeks on the Billboard charts.

question: What is the best-selling album of all time?
explanation: Thriller has sold an estimated 66–70 million copies worldwide.

question: In which decade did hip-hop emerge as a genre?
explanation: Hip-hop originated in the South Bronx, New York, in the early 1970s.

question: Which band had a famous rivalry with The Beatles in the 1960s?
explanation: The Beatles and The Rolling Stones had a famous friendly rivalry that defined British rock in the 1960s.

question: The Beatles have sold more records than any other musical act.
explanation: The Beatles have sold an estimated 600 million records worldwide.

question: Elvis Presley wrote most of his own hit songs.
explanation: Elvis rarely wrote his own material — he was primarily an interpreter of other people's songs.

question: Michael Jackson was a member of The Jackson 5 before going solo.
explanation: Michael Jackson performed with his brothers in The Jackson 5 from age 8 before launching his solo career.

question: Mozart composed his first piece of music at age 5.
explanation: Mozart began composing at age 5 and was performing for royalty across Europe by the age of 6.

question: Adele's album '21' was named after a place.
explanation: Adele named her albums after her age at the time of recording — '19', '21', '25', '30'.

question: "We Will, We Will ___ You"
explanation: Classic Queen anthem from 1977.

question: "Hello from the other ___"
explanation: Opening line of Adele's 'Hello' (2015).

question: "I Will Always Love ___"
explanation: Originally written and recorded by Dolly Parton in 1973, famously covered by Whitney Houston.

question: "Just a small town girl, living in a ___ world" — Journey
explanation: Opening lyric of 'Don't Stop Believin'' (1981) by Journey, one of the best-selling singles of all time.

question: "Is this the real life? Is this just ___?" — Queen
explanation: Opening lyric of Bohemian Rhapsody (1975) by Queen.

question: Rank these albums by release year (oldest first):
items: Dark Side of the Moon – Pink Floyd
explanation: Dark Side of the Moon (1973) → Back in Black (1980) → Thriller (1982)

question: Rank these artists by birth year (oldest first):
explanation: Elvis Presley (1935) → Beyoncé (1981) → Ed Sheeran (1991)

question: Rank these music streaming services by launch year (oldest first):
explanation: Spotify (2008) → Tidal (2014) → Apple Music (2015)

question: Rank these Taylor Swift albums by release year (oldest first):
explanation: Speak Now (2010) → 1989 (2014) → Midnights (2022)

question: Rank these music genres by approximate emergence date (oldest first):
explanation: Jazz (1900s) → Punk rock (1970s) → Hip-hop (1970s, mainstream 1980s)

question: In what year was Spotify launched?
explanation: Spotify launched in October 2008 and went public in 2018.

question: In what year did The Beatles officially break up?
explanation: The Beatles officially broke up in 1970. Paul McCartney announced his departure in April of that year.

question: How many number-one singles did Michael Jackson have in the US during his lifetime?
explanation: Michael Jackson had 13 number-one singles on the Billboard Hot 100 during his lifetime.

question: In what year was the first Grammy Awards ceremony held?
explanation: The first Grammy Awards ceremony was held on May 4, 1959, at the Beverly Hilton Hotel in California.

question: Approximately how many copies has Thriller sold worldwide (in millions)?
explanation: Thriller has sold an estimated 66–70 million copies worldwide, making it the best-selling album of all time.

question: Which city is widely considered the birthplace of jazz?
explanation: Jazz originated in New Orleans in the late 19th and early 20th centuries.

question: Which artist is known as the 'King of Pop'?
explanation: Michael Jackson earned the title 'King of Pop' for his global influence on music, dance, and fashion.

question: How many members were in The Beatles?
explanation: The Beatles had four members: John Lennon, Paul McCartney, George Harrison, and Ringo Starr.

question: Which instrument does a violinist play?
explanation: The violin is a string instrument — the player draws a bow across strings or plucks them.

question: Eminem is from which US city?
explanation: Eminem (Marshall Mathers) was born and raised in Detroit, Michigan.

question: Who sang 'Rolling in the Deep'?
explanation: Adele released 'Rolling in the Deep' in 2010 — it became one of the best-selling singles of all time.

question: What band was Freddie Mercury the lead singer of?
explanation: Freddie Mercury was the lead vocalist of the British rock band Queen, formed in London in 1970.

question: Name the Canadian artist behind 'Starboy' and 'Save Your Tears'.
explanation: The Weeknd (Abel Tesfaye) is a Canadian singer known for R&B hits like Blinding Lights and Save Your Tears.

question: Which rapper's real name is Shawn Carter?
explanation: Jay-Z (Shawn Corey Carter) is one of the best-selling music artists of all time with over 100 million records sold.

question: What instrument is associated with musician Elton John?
explanation: Elton John is renowned for his piano playing — he learned to play by ear at age 3 and got a scholarship to the Royal Academy of Music at 11.
